titleStmt.title: Letter from John Earls to the staff of the Municipal Technical Institute, Belfast, 11 March 1919
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Earls

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Forwards a letter of thanks written by Mr Vincent G. Forth. Forth expresses gratitude on the part of his mother, himself, and his siblings for a motion passed by the staff in memory of Francis C. Forth.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

correspAction.persName: staff of the Municipal Technical Institute, Belfast

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Municipal Technical Institute,
BELFAST : 11th March 1919, MEMO from Vice-Principal
To
Members of Staff. With reference to the Asselution of sympathy with
Mrs. Forthand family in their recent sad bereavement which was
passed at the Meeting of the Institute Staff on Monday, 3rd
March, 1919, I beg to forward the following letter of thanks
which has been received in reply fromMr. Vincent G. Forth. Ravenwood,
Knockbreda Park, BELFAST.
5th March 1919. Dear Professor Earls,
On behalf of my Mother I wish to thank you for
your letter of 3rd inst., enclosing copy of the Resolution
passed at the Meeting of the Permanent Staff and the Occasional
Teachers of the Municipal Technical Institute, Belfast. My Mother is deeply touched by the expression of
sympathy extended to her, in her great sorrow and bereavement,
by the Institute Staff and will be most grateful if you will be
good enough to convey to them her heartfelt thanks: at the same
time expressing my own thanks and those of my sisters and brother. My Mother takes this opportunity of expressing her
deep appreciation of the most beautiful floral tribute sent by
the Institute Staff. Yours sincerely,
(Sd). VINCENT G. FORTH. Professor Earls,
Municipal Technical Institute,
BELFAST
